steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The first sentence states caffeic acid benefits the mushroom. The second sentence adds another beneficiary—humans. This is an additive relationship, not a contrast or conclusion.

steps.title: Apply the signal
steps.content:
The logical relationship is addition. The blank needs a transition signaling that the acid's benefit to humans is an additional finding beyond its benefit to the mushroom.
  • Choice A — "Conversely,": signals opposition or contrast, but both sentences describe benefits of the same acid, so fails. ✗
  • Choice B — "Moreover,": signals an additional related point, fitting the expansion from mushroom benefits to human benefits, so fits. ✓
  • Choice C — "Rather,": signals correction or replacement of the prior idea, but nothing is being corrected here, so fails. ✗
  • Choice D — "For example,": signals a specific instance of a general claim; humans aren't an example of mushrooms, so fails. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
